message:
fix(reactor): update epoll reactor for polling 3.x API and reduce per-poll allocations

Three distinct improvements to the epoll reactor:

1. Safety: Wrap poller.add() in an unsafe block as required by polling
   3.x — the caller must guarantee the BorrowedFd remains valid for the
   lifetime of the registration.

2. API migration: Pass BorrowedFd by value (not reference) to
   poller.modify() and poller.delete(), matching the polling 3.x
   signatures that accept impl AsRawFd rather than &impl AsRawFd.

3. Allocation: Replace the per-poll Vec<PollEvent> allocation with a
   reusable Mutex<PollEvents> buffer (initial capacity 64). The buffer
   is grown only when the requested capacity exceeds the current one,
   otherwise it is cleared and reused. This eliminates a heap allocation
   on every poll() call in the hot path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
